The populated-tenant-admin view (separate snapshot) is clean BusinessStateCard, but primary (the main view the tenant-admin lands on) still shows raw AdminCrudPanel.

blockers: primary-tenant-admin renders raw AdminCrudPanel with destructive Delete action + 'Admin-facing' label + 'Raw index browser (admin debug)' copy + generic 'ui-NNNNN · active · created via UI form' rows. tenant-admin must receive a warehouse/pipeline/dataset management surface (pipeline state rail, dataset health chips, ingestion schedule, export queue) — not a generic dynamic CRUD table with hard Delete buttons on engineering-id rows.

concerns: primary-platform-admin.png missing admin sidebar chrome (page content starts at x=0). Verify whether this is a render-timing issue in snapshot or an intentional full-bleed; platform-support and populated-platform-admin show the sidebar normally.

Engineering identifiers (stack slugs, rule IDs, spec IDs) visible. Acceptable for platform-admin role managing governance, but consider humanizing rule IDs behind an aria-label if surface is ever widened to tenant-admin.

axis_summary.A_shell: PASS — admin shell on admin roles; tenant-user has kiosk/module (no engine sidebar visible).
axis_summary.B_role: PASS — platform-admin shows Durable Saga Dashboard with live 'Saga: saga-sample-payment-001' execution rail (Reserve inventory Completed / Charge payment method Completed / Dispatch confirmation email Running) + Saga Type dropdown + Step Count + blue 'Execute Saga' CTA (enabled). platform-support sees same dashboard with read-only banner + 'Escalate to a platform-admin' link + disabled grey Execute Saga button. tenant-admin sees the saga rail with a tenant-scoped read-only banner + 'Contact support for help with stuck sagas' + disabled Execute button. tenant-user sees humanized 'Your in-flight operations' page with plain-English copy ('Operations that depend on several systems (payments, orders, delivery) show their progress here while they run. This view is a summary — the platform handles retries, compensation, and timeouts for you.') + empty-state 'No operations in progress for your account' with supportive description + 'Contact support' fallback link.
axis_summary.D_state: PASS — saga execution rail shows meaningful step-by-step state (Completed/Running chips, ordered step list). tenant-user empty state is handled with gentle explanatory copy.
axis_summary.E_human: CONCERN — 'saga-sample-payment-001' identifier is visible on tenant-admin and platform-support surfaces. The string 'Saga:' label and its raw identifier leak. tenant-user correctly avoids this by using 'Your in-flight operations' framing — so the pattern proves the engine can humanize, but tenant-admin/platform-support haven't been humanized yet. Also 'Saga Type' / 'Step Count' / 'Payment Saga' are engineering-concept labels for what tenant-admin might call 'Transaction flow' / 'Steps' / 'Payment transaction'.
axis_summary.F_layer4: PASS — execution-rail grammar on admin; KIOSK grammar on tenant-user
axis_summary.F_layer5: PASS — step-state rail + status chips are signature patterns

concerns: 'Saga: saga-sample-payment-001' internal identifier visible on tenant-admin and platform-support surfaces. Humanize to something like 'Payment transaction PT-001' or hide the raw saga ID behind a tooltip.

'Saga Type' / 'Step Count' / 'Payment Saga' are engineering-concept labels on the form. tenant-admin is read-only so form is disabled, but labels still read as developer-speak. Consider 'Transaction flow' / 'Number of steps' / 'Payment transaction' or hide the form entirely on tenant-admin (read-only can be list-only).

Both tenant-admin and platform-support see the same form with the same disabled CTA — consider differentiating platform-support as full-investigation with retry/compensate controls (currently they have identical UI as tenant-admin).

crucial_blockers: data-warehouse-analytics primary-tenant-admin still shows raw AdminCrudPanel with destructive Delete actions, 'Admin-facing' label, and generic 'Raw index browser (admin debug)' copy. Populated view was humanized but the primary landing view was not. tenant-admin needs a purpose-built warehouse/pipeline domain surface replacing the AdminCrudPanel shown as primary.
